evals.prompt: Design a Docker Compose configuration for local testing that includes PostgreSQL, Redis, and a seeding service
anti_patterns: no healthchecks on services
depends_on without condition: service_healthy
POSTGRES_PASSWORD as plain text without noting it's test-only
no database setup in environment section

evals.prompt: Explain how to achieve environment parity between your CI and production environments, identifying potential red flags
expected_patterns: Same engine AND version OR database-engine
hardcoded OR undocumented OR manual setup
anti_patterns: ignoring database engine differences
no mention of config management
no discussion of auth matching
leaving manual setup steps undocumented

evals.prompt: Set up MSW (Mock Service Worker) to stub third-party API calls for Stripe, SendGrid, and a geocoding service in tests
anti_patterns: no setup or teardown
onUnhandledRequest: 'ignore'
stubbing internal modules instead of HTTP boundary
hardcoded API responses without request handling

evals.prompt: Design a preview environment strategy for per-PR deployments that auto-creates and tears down environments on GitHub Actions
expected_patterns: docker compose OR Vercel OR Cloudflare Pages OR Railway
anti_patterns: Long-lived preview environments without teardown
no automated cleanup
hardcoded URLs instead of generated namespace
no isolation between PR environments

evals.prompt: Write a shell script that resets a staging database, runs migrations, and seeds test data in an idempotent way
anti_patterns: no error handling or set flags
no idempotency (running twice causes failure)
manual SQL steps not scripted
no health verification after reset
production data copy instead of seeded data

evals.prompt: Design a multi-stage Dockerfile that includes separate targets for development, testing, and production. Explain why this pattern supports test environments
expected_patterns: AS test OR AS development OR AS seed
RUN npm ci OR npm install
anti_patterns: single monolithic stage without separation
no test-specific stage
copying production secrets or configs into test stage
test dependencies in production image

evals.prompt: Describe how to use MinIO as an S3 substitute in Docker Compose for local testing and configure an S3 client to point to it
anti_patterns: no healthcheck on MinIO service
no configuration of endpoint in code
forcing real AWS S3 in local tests
hardcoded credentials without using env vars

evals.prompt: Create an integration test script that spins up Docker Compose, runs tests, and cleans up properly on exit using a trap
expected_patterns: docker compose up -d OR docker compose -f.*up
vitest OR jest OR npm test
anti_patterns: no cleanup or manual cleanup step
no trap for cleanup on failure
dangling containers left after test exit
hard-coded URLs instead of env vars

evals.prompt: Explain the difference between local dev, CI, preview, staging, and production environments. For each, describe the data strategy and lifecycle
expected_patterns: local dev: seeded fixtures OR minimal OR fast
CI: ephemeral OR per-run OR destroyed
Created on PR OR destroyed on close OR auto-teardown
staging: long-lived OR production-like OR reset
Permanent OR Real integrations OR Live users
anti_patterns: blurring boundaries between environments
production data copies in test/staging
no mention of data lifecycle or management
same data strategy for all environments

evals.prompt: Design a strategy for stubbing external dependencies (payment, email, auth, storage) across local, CI, and staging. Explain when to use MSW vs. sandbox credentials vs. containers
expected_patterns: sandbox OR test mode OR sk_test_
HTTP boundary OR stub at boundary
anti_patterns: stubbing internal modules instead of HTTP boundary
no contract testing to verify stub accuracy
using real API credentials in non-prod
no differentiation between local/CI/staging strategies
long-lived stubs that drift from real API
